title: phaseolus vulgaris var. sphaeroleucus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. sphaeroleucus, also known as the bush bean or dwarf bean, is a variety of the common bean plant (Phaseolus vulgaris) characterized by its compact growth and spherical pods. The plant contains medicinal properties, including isoflavones, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of consuming bush beans include improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of certain cancers, and support for bone health. Bush beans can be prepared as herbal teas, tinctures, or dried powders, and are also commonly used in cooking and as a food source.
